display.purpose: Marketing H1 and large section titles.

body.purpose: UI and long-form reading; prefer max 65 to 75ch for articles.

shadows.name: primitive-rest
shadows.purpose: Cards and default shadcn buttons at rest.

shadows.name: hero-stat
shadows.purpose: Rose-tinted lift for hero metric panels only.

motion.name: ease-colors
motion.value: color 150ms ease, background-color 150ms ease, border-color 150ms ease
motion.purpose: Default transition-colors feel on buttons and links.

motion.name: nav-hover
motion.purpose: Navbar ghost buttons use transition-all duration-300.

components.name: Marketing primary button
components.description: Pill-shaped consult CTA as used in hero and sections.
components.css: .ds-btn-brand { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; min-height: 56px; padding: 0 32px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #DA5F6F; color: #ffffff; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; text-decoration: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-brand:hover { background: #C54B5B; } .ds-btn-brand:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #171717; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: UI default button
components.description: shadcn default variant for dense UI and Stripe contexts.
components.css: .ds-btn-ui { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; background: #171717; color: #fafafa; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border: none; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease, opacity 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-ui:hover { background: rgb(23 23 23 / 0.9); } .ds-btn-ui:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #171717; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-ui:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Default marketing and pricing card shell.
components.css: .ds-card { border-radius: 12px; border: 1px solid #e5e5e5; background: #ffffff; color: #0a0a0a; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05); } .ds-card-body { padding: 24px; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Text input
components.description: Default bordered field for calculators and settings.
components.css: .ds-input { display: flex; width: 100%; height: 36px; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #e5e5e5; background: transparent; color: #0a0a0a; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #737373; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px #0a0a0a; } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Navbar pill
components.description: Fixed centered nav with blur, translucent background, rose hover on ghost items.
components.css: .ds-nav { max-width: 90%; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 16px; height: 64px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; border-radius: 24px; border: 1px solid #e5e5e5; background: rgb(255 255 255 / 0.8); backdrop-filter: blur(16px); box-shadow: 0 10px 15px -3px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1), 0 4px 6px -4px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1); } .ds-nav-inner { font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.5rem; color: #DA5F6F; }

narrative.overview:
Nutralech pairs a human, approachable presence with rigorous clarity: food, body, and habits discussed like at a trusted table, not in a sales funnel. The interface stacks a committed rose accent on warm white and whisper-pink atmospheres so marketing pages feel inviting without abandoning structure. Under that layer, shadcn-style primitives keep forms, dialogs, and Stripe flows legible: near-black semantic primary, crisp borders, and restrained shadows.

The system rejects interchangeable SaaS hero patterns, decorative glass without intent, and category-reflex wellness teal unless explicitly reintroduced with a named reference. Depth is mostly lift-on-hover cards and diffuse rose-tinted shadows on white shells, not heavy skeuomorphism.
keyCharacteristics: Marketing accent is brand rose, not the semantic primary token used by default shadcn buttons.
Serif display for section heroes; Inter for UI chrome and long reading.
Pill navigation and pill CTAs on landing; md radius on system inputs and default buttons.
Atmosphere uses vertical washes and large soft blurs behind photography, used sparingly so tools stay calm.

rules.name: The Two-Primary Rule
rules.body: Marketing surfaces may lead with Brand Rose. App-style components should respect semantic primary unless the design task explicitly overrides for a campaign.

rules.name: The Wash Pair Rule
rules.body: Section backgrounds that use atmosphere gradients should move between white and a single blush tint. Do not stack unrelated gradient stops for novelty.

rules.name: The Serif Containment Rule
rules.body: Serif display sizes are for marketing headings and hero moments. Do not set long body copy in all-caps serif tracking unless that surface is intentionally editorial.

rules.name: The Flat Forms Rule
rules.body: Calculators and checkout paths should stay tonally flat: rely on borders and focus rings, not marketing blurs, behind inputs.

dos: Do lead marketing CTAs with Brand Rose on white or blush wash, with pill shape when the action is consult booking or equivalent primary conversion.
Do keep Inter for form labels, calculator copy, and Stripe-adjacent UI.
Do use focus-visible ring and visible borders so keyboard paths meet accessibility targets.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion for decorative pulses and ambient blurs when adding new motion.
donts: Don't use twin stat cards with huge numbers and tiny labels as the only hero story; PRODUCT.md rejects generic SaaS landing patterns that read as interchangeable AI output.
Don't stack decorative glass and gradient chrome without intent; glass is for rare emphasis, not every kicker.
Don't default to stock healthcare teal clichés unless deliberately chosen with a named reference (PRODUCT.md).
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on prose callouts; audit mdx-components blockquote pattern when tightening the system.
Don't imply medical diagnosis through visual urgency outside the destructive token role for real errors.
